touches.ai_summary: Cold email sent to Jackson Kim introducing AskElephant's AI note-taking capabilities for customer success teams. No response received.
LinkedIn connection request sent to Jackson Kim with brief message about pipeline visibility challenges in EdTech. No response received.
Follow-up LinkedIn message sharing case study of similar EdTech company improving CS efficiency. No response received.
Second cold email with subject line focused on reducing manual note-taking for CS teams. No response received.
LinkedIn message asking if Jackson had 15 minutes to discuss pipeline visibility challenges. No response received.
Final outreach email offering specific value prop around automated call summaries for CS workflows. No response received.
Jackson replied to previous email expressing interest in learning more about pipeline visibility features. Brief exchange confirmed his team is currently evaluating solutions.

questions_asked.question: How does AskElephant differ from Fireflies?
questions_asked.answer: AskElephant focuses on structured deal insights and pipeline visibility, while Fireflies is primarily transcription-focused.

touches.ai_summary: Jackson confirmed availability for discovery call and mentioned involving other stakeholders if initial conversation goes well. Shared they have 23 CS team members who could benefit.
next_steps: Discovery call scheduled for next week

touches.ai_summary: Discovery call with Jackson covered current CS workflows and pain points around pipeline visibility. He mentioned they're comparing multiple tools including Fireflies. Team struggles with manual note-taking across 23 CS reps.

questions_asked.question: Can AskElephant integrate with our existing CRM and Slack?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we have native integrations with major CRMs and Slack for automated updates.

questions_asked.question: What's your current process for capturing customer calls?
questions_asked.answer: Mostly manual notes in various formats, inconsistent quality.

questions_asked.question: What's your pricing model for mid-market teams?
questions_asked.answer: We price per seat with tiered automation features, typically $850-900 per seat annually.

next_steps: Schedule follow-up to discuss use cases

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up call dove deeper into SageForm's specific use case around pipeline visibility. Jackson confirmed Riley Lewis (COO) would need to approve any purchase. Discussed automation tier options.

questions_asked.question: Who else should be involved in the evaluation process?
questions_asked.answer: Riley Lewis our COO will make final decision, and Priya Martin will do technical evaluation.

questions_asked.question: How long does implementation typically take?
questions_asked.answer: Usually 1-2 weeks for full team onboarding with our CS support.

next_steps: Introduce Priya Martin for technical evaluation

touches.ai_summary: Brief check-in call. Jackson confirmed Priya's availability for technical deep-dive and mentioned timeline is flexible but hoping to decide within 4-6 weeks.

questions_asked.question: What's driving the timeline on your end?
questions_asked.answer: Fiscal year planning, want to have tool in place before Q3.

touches.ai_summary: Quick call to confirm technical eval logistics and answer questions about data security. Jackson mentioned competitor evaluation is ongoing.

questions_asked.question: Is your data storage SOC 2 compliant?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we're SOC 2 Type II certified with data encryption at rest and in transit.

touches.ai_summary: Pre-technical eval call with Jackson to align on evaluation criteria. Discussed unlimited automation tier benefits for their team size. He mentioned Fireflies quote came in lower but lacks some features.

questions_asked.question: What features are most critical for Priya's evaluation?
questions_asked.answer: API access, custom fields, and bulk export capabilities.

next_steps: Prepare technical eval materials for Priya

touches.ai_summary: Technical evaluation call with Priya Martin. Walked through API documentation, custom field configuration, and bulk data export. She tested integration with their CRM sandbox. Positive reception to automation workflows.

questions_asked.question: Can we customize which fields are auto-populated in our CRM?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, fully configurable field mapping through the admin console.

questions_asked.question: What's the API rate limit?
questions_asked.answer: 10,000 requests per hour on unlimited tier, expandable if needed.

questions_asked.question: Do these capabilities meet your technical requirements?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, this looks solid. I'll write up my evaluation for Jackson and Riley.

next_steps: Priya to complete technical evaluation report

touches.ai_summary: Email follow-up to discovery discussions. Shared case study of EdTech customer with similar team size. Jackson acknowledged receipt and said Priya's eval was positive.
Email confirming demo logistics and attendees. Jackson mentioned Riley might join second demo depending on schedule. Requested focus on pipeline visibility dashboards.

questions_asked.question: Can you show real-time deal health scoring during the demo?
questions_asked.answer: Absolutely, that's a key feature we'll showcase.

next_steps: Demo scheduled with Jackson and team

touches.ai_summary: Product demo focused on pipeline visibility and automated call summaries. Jackson and three CS team members attended. Positive engagement with dashboard features. Questions about reporting capabilities.

questions_asked.question: Can managers see roll-up reports across their team?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, hierarchical reporting by team, region, or custom segments.

questions_asked.question: How does the sentiment analysis work?
questions_asked.answer: AI-powered analysis of call tone, keywords, and discussion patterns.

questions_asked.question: Which features would be most valuable for your team?
questions_asked.answer: Automated summaries and the pipeline health dashboard stand out.

next_steps: Schedule second demo with Riley Lewis

touches.ai_summary: Second demo diving deeper into automation workflows and admin controls. Jackson's team tested live transcription. Discussed implementation timeline and onboarding process. Strong interest confirmed.

questions_asked.question: Can we pilot with a subset of users first?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we can do phased rollout starting with 5-10 users.

questions_asked.question: What level of support comes with unlimited tier?
questions_asked.answer: Dedicated CSM, priority support, and quarterly business reviews.

questions_asked.question: Are you ready to move toward a proposal?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, let's get Riley involved and discuss terms.

touches.ai_summary: Proposal review call with Jackson. Walked through $19,700 ACV for 23 seats on unlimited automation tier. He appreciated the pricing but mentioned need to compare final numbers with Fireflies offer.

questions_asked.question: Is there flexibility on the per-seat price?
questions_asked.answer: We can discuss volume discounting depending on commitment term.

questions_asked.question: Where does Fireflies pricing compare?
questions_asked.answer: They're about 15% lower but missing some automation features we need.

objections: Need to justify price premium over Fireflies to Riley
next_steps: Executive meeting with Riley Lewis scheduled

touches.ai_summary: Executive meeting with Riley Lewis and Jackson Kim. Presented business case focused on CS efficiency and pipeline visibility ROI. Riley engaged positively, asked about implementation risk and vendor stability.

questions_asked.question: What's your customer retention rate?
questions_asked.answer: 96% annual retention, with strong references in EdTech vertical.

questions_asked.question: How do you ensure data privacy for student-related calls?
questions_asked.answer: FERPA-compliant data handling with configurable retention policies.

questions_asked.question: What concerns do you have about moving forward?
questions_asked.answer: Mainly price relative to alternatives and ensuring team adoption.

next_steps: Jackson and Riley to discuss internally

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up proposal call with Jackson. He confirmed Riley is supportive but wants to explore contract terms and potential discount. Discussed annual vs. multi-year commitment options.

questions_asked.question: Would a multi-year deal help with internal approval?
questions_asked.answer: Possibly, if we can get better pricing on a 2-year term.

touches.ai_summary: Negotiation call discussing contract terms and pricing adjustments. Jackson pushed for 10% discount citing Fireflies competitive pricing. Discussed value differentiation and ROI justification.

questions_asked.question: Can you match Fireflies on price?
questions_asked.answer: Our feature set justifies premium, but we can explore volume discount on multi-year term.

questions_asked.question: What discount level would get this approved by Riley?
questions_asked.answer: 7-10% would make this an easy yes from our end.

next_steps: Discuss discount approval with sales leadership

touches.ai_summary: Brief negotiation call was rescheduled due to Jackson's scheduling conflict. Confirmed continued interest and agreed to reconnect later in week.
Second executive meeting with Riley Lewis. Presented revised proposal with competitive analysis vs. Fireflies. Riley focused on long-term value and team adoption plan. Positive tone overall.

questions_asked.question: What happens if we need to add more seats mid-contract?
questions_asked.answer: Pro-rated additions at same per-seat rate, true-up at renewal.

questions_asked.question: Can we get a customer reference in EdTech?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we'll connect you with two similar-sized customers.

questions_asked.question: What would get you comfortable signing this month?
questions_asked.answer: Final pricing clarity and seeing those references.

touches.ai_summary: Email follow-up sharing two EdTech customer references and case studies. Jackson confirmed he and Riley reviewed and both conversations went well. Ready to finalize terms.
Negotiation call was rescheduled to include Riley Lewis for final pricing discussion. Jackson indicated they're close to decision and want to align on terms this week.
next_steps: Rescheduled pricing call with Riley and Jackson

touches.ai_summary: Final pricing call with Jackson and Riley. Agreed to 8% discount on 2-year term bringing ACV to $19,700 for 23 seats on unlimited automation tier. Riley confirmed budget approval and legal review to follow.

questions_asked.question: When can we start onboarding?
questions_asked.answer: Immediately after contract signature, typically 1-2 week implementation.

questions_asked.question: Any remaining concerns before we move to contracts?
questions_asked.answer: No, we're aligned. Let's get this to legal.
